text: Founders with deep technical expertise from one industry possess a unique advantage when applying it to disrupt a different, more traditional sector.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Founders with long careers managing small, slow-growth companies often lack the operational experience and mindset required to navigate the distinct challenges of achieving venture-backed hyper-growth and scale.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: A founder's prior success leading a company to a significant exit or IPO is the strongest predictor of their ability to achieve repeatable, large-scale success.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Founders without significant, non-founding operational or industry experience have a lower probability of scaling a company to a large, successful outcome.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Leadership experience in an adjacent service industry is a weak predictor of success in capital-intensive manufacturing, often indicating gaps in critical operational and go-to-market expertise.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Prioritize founders with deep executive experience in their target industry, especially when combined with a prior successful company exit, as this de-risks both market and execution challenges.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: A long history of founding small businesses is a negative predictor for achieving the hyper-growth and massive scale required for top-tier venture outcomes.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: General management experience is a negative signal when not paired with deep, recent, and relevant technical expertise required to innovate and scale at venture speed.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Founders with careers limited to sales leadership at large companies often lack the crucial product-building and zero-to-one skills required for early-stage success.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Prioritize founders with deep, sustained expertise in their specific domain. A scattered professional history is a primary indicator of a fatal founder-market fit gap, correlating with failure.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Prioritize founders with a robust professional network (2000+ LinkedIn connections), indicating strong macro-market influence and effective access to capital/talent within high-growth digital micro-segments, especially when combined with deep technical expertise.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Founders in the 'Clinical & Diagnostic Healthcare' micro-market with a moderate professional network (e.g., 1000-2000 LinkedIn connections) are high risk if their network is primarily composed of academic peers and research collaborators, lacking robust connections to healthcare industry commercial leaders, experienced healthtech investors, or serial entrepreneurs.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Founders possessing diverse advanced degrees (e.g., MBA combined with PhD/MS in Biological Engineering, Food Science, or Functional Genomics), who demonstrate 6+ years of senior R&D or scientific leadership roles within large enterprises (500+ employees) in Biotechnology, and possessing a robust professional network (2000-5000 LinkedIn connections).
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Favor companies operating in macro-markets experiencing significant secular tailwinds, such as resource demand in Extractive Industries, operational efficiency drives, or platform economy growth.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Favor founders whose professional experience includes venture capital or private equity roles, providing an investor-centric perspective and strategic understanding of funding and exit strategies.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: Founders must have at least 2 years of direct technical contribution (e.g., Software Engineer, Research Scientist) within core technology stacks of high-growth micro-markets (e.g., AI/ML, SaaS Platforms, Cybersecurity), demonstrating critical impact on product development or foundational innovation.
notes: 16-policy v25 ensemble; full ensemble CV F0.5 = 0.294

text: This founder has previously overseen or been involved in at least one acquisition as a founder.
This founder held a senior or executive role (Director, VP, GM, C-suite, or equivalent) at a large company (1,000+ employees) before founding their current startup.
This founder has a PhD or postdoc from a top-tier university (QS rank 20 or better) in a technical or scientific field.
This founder is a repeat founder — they have previously founded or co-founded at least one other company before their current startup.
This founder has deep domain expertise: their professional experience is concentrated in the same industry as their current startup, with 5 or more years of relevant experience in that industry.
This founder held a C-level role (CEO, CTO, CRO, CMO, COO, or equivalent) at a company with 200 or more employees.
This founder has professional experience in venture capital, private equity, angel investing, or as a professional investor.
This founder has an IPO (initial public offering) in their track record, either as a founder or as a senior executive at the company that went public.
This founder spent 10 or more years at a single company without reaching Director level or above.

This founder's most senior roles are in back-office, support, QA, or non-core functions (for example: QA lead, SDET, customer success manager, program manager, administrative roles) rather than product, engineering, research, or executive leadership.
This founder's primary professional experience is in consulting, agencies, freelancing, or professional services rather than building products or technology companies.
This founder has multiple short stints (less than 2 years each) across unrelated industries, suggesting an unfocused or scattered career path.
This founder's current startup is in an industry that is completely unrelated to any of their prior professional experience or education.
This founder's highest degree is from a low-ranked institution (QS rank 200+) AND they have no other strong compensating signals such as senior leadership roles, prior exits, or C-level experience.
This founder has only held junior-level roles throughout their career (analyst, intern, associate, junior engineer) with no progression to senior or leadership positions.
This founder has a degree from an elite university (QS rank 10 or better).
This founder has 10 or more years of total professional experience (sum of all roles listed).
This founder shows fast career velocity: they progressed from individual contributor roles to Director, VP, or C-level within roughly 10 years of starting their career.
This founder's current startup is in a technology-related industry (such as software, AI, cybersecurity, biotech, data analytics, cloud computing, robotics, or similar).
This founder has worked at a very large company with 10,000 or more employees.
